steps.title: Identify the key signal in the researchers' argument
steps.content: The strongest signal is the researchers' statement: “Earth’s climate is changing in ways that challenge such classifications.” This directly indicates that the invasive classification may not hold as climate changes.

steps.title: Apply this to each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: Introduces unsupported history — that rainbow lorikeets were previously present in New Zealand and were outcompeted. The passage contains no such information. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: Reverses the passage’s premise by assuming climate change does not affect classifications. This directly contradicts the researchers’ emphasis on climate change challenging existing labels. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: Correctly captures the logical consequence — if climate will change environmental conditions, then the invasive label reflects conditions that may not persist. Supported.
  • Choice D: Proposes selectively applying invasive labels to some species but not others. The passage questions the classification framework generally, not selectively. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
